title: Incorrectly Implemented Lucas-Lehmer Primality Test for Mersenne Numbers

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: The Lucas-Lehmer primality test is a method used to determine whether a Mersenne number is prime or not. However, there are certain optimizations that can be made in the implementation to improve efficiency

editorial.title: Optimizing Lucas-Lehmer Primality Test Implementation
editorial.intuition: The Lucas-Lehmer primality test involves a recursive formula that calculates the value of the sequence until it reaches either 0 or 1, signifying primality or non-primality respectively. However, this formula can be optimized using bitwise operations to reduce the time complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using bitwise operations to calculate the power of 2 modulo (2^p - 1) at each step, which greatly reduces the number of iterations required.

functionSignature.java: public boolean isPrime(int p, int M);
functionSignature.python: def is_prime(p: int, M: int) -> bool:
